mode_selected_reason: Dispatcher quick-check found 3 git-owned files STALE on FUSE (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html) — the same class of drift flagged on 2026-04-19 and 2026-04-20. Both prior Mode 2 runs explicitly said the fix belongs in Mode 4: write PROP-010 (reverse/post-push git→workspace sync). Running Mode 2 a third day in a row would just re-cp the files and defer the architectural fix. This run: (a) applied the mechanical cp self-fix inline, (b) wrote PROP-010 with full file-level patches for build.js + decider prompt. Mode selection logic rule 3 ('previous report has unresolved findings needing fixes → Mode 4') explicitly permits this override.

pipeline_health.details: All scheduled agents producing on cadence. Poller last ran 2026-04-21T00:13Z (~10h ago, 12h cycle). Analyst attention-inbox 35min old. Curmudgeon tracker.json 2min old (very fresh). Decider daily-report 04:29Z, additional run 08:31Z (2h BAU cycle, 25 commits in last 24h). Integrity 07:09Z. Social last daily-report 2026-04-20T11:13Z (24h ago, next ~11:05Z today). 71 WINs. Curmudgeon queue depth 4 (healthy).

infrastructure.details: 3 git-owned files STALE on FUSE workspace vs GitHub main: data/wins.json (ws cc7faa2a → gh d80899e9), data/sections.json (ws 1b142b33 → gh fad74b7d), docs/index.html (ws 9a23f106 → gh abd2b32b). Workspace index.html was pinned at commit b4fa494; main at df4b231. Three decider commits behind: df4b231 (SEC-5.0.c2 review), 67b14d5 (EXP-195+196 self-apply), b4fa494 (9 patches). Root cause identical to 2026-04-19 and 2026-04-20 runs — decider commits via `git push` without calling `build.js publish`, so FUSE never sees the new data between publishes. Self-fixed via direct cp from fresh clone to workspace for all 3 files; post-fix md5 matches GitHub raw. Auth OK (PAT 93 chars, api.github.com returns 200). CI last run green. Disk 82% (1794MB free) — flat for 9 days at 81-83%, still above WARNING threshold but trend stable.

cost_engineering.details: Deferred; Mode 4 took this run. Previous Mode 3 findings (CLAUDE.md 148L, per-agent context loads) are still broadly valid — no prompt-size mutations since last Mode 3.

proposals_and_fixes.details: PROP-010 written with 4 file-level edits (3 in build.js, 1 in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md). Inline self-fix applied for FUSE drift (3 files, cp from clone). No other self-fixable items found this run.

Decider curmudgeon queue is at 4 items today so the rev-loop pressure the PROP addresses is currently low. Reiterated as open.

Recommend the PROP author tighten the verification_pattern to drop the CLEAN_CLONE env-var gate — it's gating on a variable that no tinker run sets.

dome_social_2026-04-19.note: No further observations needed; social ran normally through 2026-04-20T11:13Z.

dome_workspace_sync_schedule.note: Cadence is 0 */4 * * * per prior report; continues to fire on schedule.

self_fixes_applied_this_run.detail: cp from /tmp/dmr-tinker (clean clone at main HEAD df4b231) to workspace: data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html. Post-fix all 3 md5 match GitHub raw. No content mutation; strictly sync git→workspace. Idempotent, zero-risk. Note: data/uncounted-failures.json and data/predictions.json md5-match main today (last decider edits were not on those files), so only 3 of the 5 usually-stale files needed the cp.
self_fixes_applied_this_run.justification: Mode 2 procedure explicitly permits; Mode 4 explicitly permits mechanical self-fixes. The fix does not compete with PROP-010 (the architectural fix) — both are needed, and the PROP is now written.

proposals_written_this_run.summary: Add a `sync-workspace` target to build.js (extract the existing sync block into a function; no logic change) so post-push callers can refresh FUSE without the HTML/PDF/commit/push overhead of `publish`. Have the decider invoke `node build.js sync-workspace` immediately after `git push origin main`. Soft-fail on the decider side so push success is never blocked. Workspace-sync's 4h cycle remains as a backstop.
proposals_written_this_run.verification_pattern: grep -q "target === 'sync-workspace'" build.js && grep -q 'syncToWorkspace()' build.js && grep -q 'node build.js sync-workspace' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
proposals_written_this_run.impact_if_applied: Eliminates the Mode 2 trigger that has fired 3 consecutive days. Cuts FUSE-drift window from up-to-4h to <1s per decider push. Zero cost to non-pushing agents.

findings.finding: Recurring FUSE drift — 3rd consecutive day, 3 git-owned files stale pre-fix (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html)
findings.root_cause: build.js publish is the only git→FUSE sync mechanism; decider self-apply commits push without calling publish. 25 commits/24h at 2h cycle means FUSE is stale for hours at a time between publishes.
findings.evidence: md5 mismatch for 3 files; workspace pinned at commit b4fa494 while main is at df4b231; drift commits df4b231, 67b14d5, b4fa494 are all decider self-applies in the last 12h. Previous tinker reports 2026-04-19 and 2026-04-20 flagged same-class drift.
findings.self_fix_applied: yes — cp 3 files from clone to workspace
findings.followup: PROP-010 written this run with full file-level patches; implementation pending

findings.finding: Open issues count jumped from 38 (2026-04-20) to 73 today — decider net +35 in ~24h
findings.root_cause: Curmudgeon review processed SEC-5.0.c2 generated 8 new issues (holes) this morning; decider opened them faster than it closed. Not a bug per se — new work from a review cycle is expected — but worth tracking. If the ratio stays above 1 for a few days, the backlog will compound.
findings.evidence: status.json: issues_closed_this_run=7, issues_created_this_run=8, open_issues=73, closed_issues=1097. Previous tinker report (2026-04-20): 38 open. Daily-report notes list ISS-1288–1295 as the newly opened issues.
findings.self_fix_applied: no — not self-fixable; this is decider/analyst workflow throughput
findings.followup: Watch for 3+ consecutive days of net-positive open issues; if so, flag for Mode 3 analysis (curmudgeon tuning or analyst capacity)

findings.finding: PROP-009r2 verification_pattern has a CLEAN_CLONE env-var gate that makes tinker-side auto-verification return STILL_BROKEN even though all factual conditions are met
findings.root_cause: Pattern uses `[ -n "${CLEAN_CLONE:-}" ] && <greps>` — when the env var is empty (which it always is from tinker's bash -c invocation), the whole pattern short-circuits to STILL_BROKEN regardless of the file contents.
findings.evidence: Manual run of the 6 grep conditions all return 0; pattern as written returns STILL_BROKEN. This is an auto-verification false positive (falsely STILL_BROKEN), not a content regression.
findings.self_fix_applied: no — modifying another PROP's verification_pattern is out of scope without explicit authorization
findings.followup: Recommend PROP-009r2 author drop the CLEAN_CLONE env gate; the grep conditions alone are sufficient. Or, tinker's verification loop can set CLEAN_CLONE=1 before running patterns as a workaround.

findings.finding: Disk at 82%, flat for 9 days; still above WARNING threshold
findings.root_cause: Clone accumulation from churn-and-burn cadence (decider 2h, curmudgeon hourly, workspace-sync 4h). Clones delete in the normal path; residual is ~2GB of system image + installed deps.
findings.followup: Tracked. No action unless a run crashes between clone and cleanup. A nightly orphan-clone reaper is still on the idea list (not proposed yet).

recommendations.recommendation: Apply PROP-010 — 4 file-level edits authored with full find/replace text. Verification pattern provided. Once applied, the Mode 2 FUSE-drift trigger disappears.

PROP-005 — 17 consecutive no-op cycles. Either close as wontfix, re-scope (maybe decider no-op detection is the right signal but 'Pre-flight Work Check' was the wrong intervention), or the problem is not actually a problem and we should stop escalating.
PROP-008 gates — curmudgeon rev-loop dampening. Apply when curmudgeon queue pressure rises again; currently depth-4 so not urgent.
Tighten PROP-009r2 verification_pattern — drop the CLEAN_CLONE env-var gate. Current pattern is falsely reporting STILL_BROKEN on every tinker run.

Watch open-issues count. If net-positive for 3 more days, deep-dive in Mode 3 — is curmudgeon over-generating, is decider under-closing, or is this a healthy discovery burst from the recent SEC-5.0 review?

counter_arguments.claim: Overriding the dispatcher (Mode 2 says 'FUSE red → Mode 2') to run Mode 4 is dispatcher-bypass and should be discouraged.
counter_arguments.counter: The dispatcher's mode selection logic explicitly lists 'If previous report has unresolved findings needing fixes → Mode 4' as rule 3, ABOVE the generic 'anything red → that mode' rule. Running Mode 2 a third consecutive day would be a fidelity-to-the-letter move that defeats the spirit of the dispatcher — it literally says 'when you have a specific PROP owed, write it.' The override was documented in mode_selected_reason, and the Mode 2 self-fix was still applied inline so nothing downstream is worse off. This is exactly what the rule was written for.

counter_arguments.claim: Writing a PROP with ~200 lines of patch content is over-engineering; a terse 'refactor the sync block and call it from decider' note would let the next human implementer figure it out.
counter_arguments.counter: Prior tinker runs explicitly demanded 'be specific enough to apply directly, write the actual files not descriptions' (tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md Step 2, quality rules). PROP-010 follows that rule literally — every find/replace is byte-accurate, the verification_pattern is testable, the counter-arguments section pre-empts the obvious objections. A terse PROP-010 is what the prior 3 cycles produced in the report body (as 'recommendation: write PROP-010') and nobody wrote it. Specificity is the point.

counter_arguments.claim: The self-fix (cp 3 files) plus writing the PROP in the same run is doing two things; per 'one run one mode deep work', this is shallow.
counter_arguments.counter: The self-fix is 30 seconds of cp work and is mechanically invariant — it does not compete for attention with authoring the PROP. The PROP is the deep work of this run; the self-fix is the hygiene that the PROP itself documents (Mode 2 procedure Step 1 permits it). Skipping the self-fix to preserve mode-purity would leave FUSE stale for 4 more hours until workspace-sync backfilled, and integrity at 07:09Z already ran against stale HTML (no issue observed, but the risk is real). The 'one mode' rule is about avoiding shallow-checklist-mode sweeps, not about refusing to press Ctrl-C to kill a smoke alarm while writing a fire prevention memo.

counter_arguments.claim: PROP-010 adds ~100ms to every decider push, ~20× more sync-block executions per day. What if the sync block has a latent bug that manifests at 20× but not 2×?
counter_arguments.counter: The sync block is being extracted verbatim — no logic change. Every line of the function is byte-identical to what publish has been running for weeks without incident (verified by grep against current build.js). The extra 100ms × 20 = ~2s/day of CPU; on a sandbox where decider runs take minutes, this is noise. If a latent bug exists, it has existed for weeks and publishing more often exposes it faster, which is a debug advantage. Also: the soft-fail (`|| echo WARN`) means a bug logs a warning and leaves FUSE in whatever state it's in; push success is never blocked.
